concise_definition: Back to top - Abbreviations : Comprises a shortened form of one or more letters, which could represent certain basketball-related terms or phrases such as names of player positions, statistics, strategies or tactics.

sections.heading: Loop offense
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of re-screen and fill action followed by loop action within 1-4 low offense.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of re-screen and fill action followed by loop action within 1-4 low offense.]

Loop offenseconsists of strategies and/or general tactics that involve loop action to create scoring opportunities around the perimeter areas of the court or near the basket.

sections.heading: Youth basketball passing drills
sections.text: Youth basketball passing drillsare practice exercises that can help young players develop throwing and catching techniques while also emphasizing hand-eye coordination.

sections.heading: 5 out backdoor offense
sections.text: 5 out backdoor offensecomprise backdoor cuts that could be utilized as a counter to create a high percentage scoring opportunity near the basket within the overall 5 out motion strategy.

sections.heading: Horns backdoor offense
sections.text: Horns backdoor offensecomprises actions that are designed to create a scoring opportunity near the basket through the use of a backdoor cut via Horns.

sections.heading: 5 out offense entries
sections.text: 5 out offense entriesgenerally emphasize high percentage scoring opportunities such as open three-point shots or layups at the rim from initial actions, prior to flowing swiftly into the 5 out motion strategy.

sections.heading: Box dribble entry offense
sections.text: Box dribble entry offensecomprises actions from the box set that focuses on creating possible scoring opportunities near the basket or around perimeter areas of the court after a player initially dribbles the basketball towards a wing area, usually from the top.

sections.heading: Horns stagger screen offense
sections.text: Horns stagger screen offensecomprises actions initiated from the Horns set that involve the stagger screen to create one or more scoring opportunities near the basket or around perimeter areas of the court.

sections.heading: 1-4 set high post offense
sections.text: 1-4 set high post offensecomprises actions, initiated from a 1-4 high alignment, that focus on creating potential scoring opportunities near the basket or around the perimeter areas of the court after an offensive player within the high post area receives possession of the basketball.

sections.heading: Early offense
sections.text: Early offensecomprises action that occurs when a team in possession of the basketball attempts to score quickly before the opposing team is fully set and prepared to limit or prevent scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: Triangle low post offense
sections.text: Triangle low post offensefeature various actions initiated from the triangle offense that generally emphasize creating scoring opportunities once the basketball gets entered into the low post area, typically while in possession of a low post player.

sections.heading: Triangle offense counters
sections.text: Triangle offense counterscomprise different actions that could be utilized to mitigate certain defensive measures by the opposing team, ultimately to generate scoring opportunities near the basket or around perimeter areas of the court.

sections.heading: Last second inbound plays
sections.text: Last second inbound playscomprise offensive actions from a baseline or sideline throw-in that could result in scoring opportunities near the basket or around perimeter areas of the basketball court during the end of a period such as a quarter or half.

sections.heading: Horns high post offense
sections.text: Horns high post offensecomprises actions from a Horns set that emphasizes creating potential scoring opportunities near the basket or around the perimeter areas of the court once a high post player receives possession of the basketball.

sections.heading: Last second plays
sections.text: Last second basketball playscomprise offensive actions that could result in scoring opportunities near the basket or around perimeter areas of the court during the end of a period such as a quarter or half.

sections.heading: Horns low post offense
sections.text: Horns low post offensecomprises actions initiated from the Horns set that are utilized to create scoring opportunities, typically for low post players near the basket or alternatively, for offensive players in general near the low post areas.

sections.heading: Double ball screen offense
sections.text: Double ball screen offensecomprises one player in possession of the basketball who utilizes on-ball screens by two additional players within offensive action, ultimately to create scoring opportunities near the basket or around the different perimeter areas.

sections.heading: Horns quick hitter offense
sections.text: Horns quick hitter offensecomprises a brief number of actions initiated from a Horns set, ultimately to create scoring opportunities near the basket or around perimeter areas of the court.

sections.heading: 5 out continuity offense
sections.text: 5 out continuity offensecomprises actions that could be implemented and performed on either side of the floor consecutively, initially by way of five players around the perimeter areas of the court.

sections.heading: 4 out 1 in continuity offense
sections.text: 4 out 1 in continuity offenseincorporates actions that could be implemented and performed on either side of the floor consecutively, initially by way of four perimeter players around one interior player.

sections.heading: 3 out 2 in flex offense
sections.text: 3 out 2 in flex offensefeatures actions that mainly focus on screening and cutting near the vicinity of a corner area to generate high percentage scoring opportunities, initially by way of three players around the perimeter and two players near the low post areas.
